content.full_text:
P43305

STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

                                                      December 10, 1975

                                                      75-12-122

To Constitutional Officers
and Members of the Legislature:

     Recently, many elected officers have asked
the staff of the Fair Political Practices Commission
whether the disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act are applicable to holiday greeting cards.
The Commission is presently considering the mass
mailing provisions of the Act, which are determina-
tive of this issue. This letter sets forth the
interim policy which will be followed by the Commission.

     With respect to holiday greeting cards in
1975, the cost of purchasing and/or mailing such
cards need not be reported so long as it is not paid
by a campaign committee. If the cost is paid by a
committee, it must be reported as an expenditure on
the committee's campaign statement. Since, however,
many of you already have arranged to mail your cards
by means other than those prescribed in Government
Code Section 84305, the procedures set forth therein
need not be observed this year. Holiday cards sent
by or on behalf of a state candidate need not be for-
warded to the Commission this year.

     It should be emphasized that the interim policy
stated above does not necessarily reflect the conclu-
sions the Commission will reach after comprehensive
consideration of all the issues surrounding mass mail-
ings. However, any new policy adopted by the Commission
will be applied prospectively. Any suggestions you have
for treatment of mass mailings under the Political Re-
form Act will be welcomed by the Commission.

          Seasons greetings!

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DHL:jp

                                                      4

sections.question_synthetic: Are holiday greeting cards subject to disclosure provisions and mass mailing requirements under the California Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Holiday greeting card costs need not be reported if not paid by a campaign committee; if paid by a committee, they must be reported as expenditures. Compliance with Government Code Section 84305 procedures is not required for 1975, and holiday cards need not be forwarded to the Commission. This is an interim policy pending comprehensive review.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a mass guidance letter rather than a response to a specific inquiry; no traditional Question/Conclusion sections present. Interim policy nature and prospective application statement noted. Extract confidence reduced slightly due to lack of formal Q&A structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are holiday greeting cards subject to disclosure provisions and mass mailing requirements under the California Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: Holiday greeting card costs need not be reported if not paid by a campaign committee; if paid by a committee, they must be reported as expenditures. Compliance with Government Code Section 84305 procedures is not required for 1975, and holiday cards need not be forwarded to the Commission. This is an interim policy pending comprehensive review.

BROWN JR., Governor FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 1100 K Street Sacramento 95814 (916) 322-5660 December 10, 1975 75-12-122 To Constitutional Officers and Members of the Legislature: Recently, many elected officers have asked the staff of the Fair Political Practices Commission whether the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act are applicable to holiday greeting cards. The Commission is presently considering the mass mailing provisions of the Act, which are determina- tive of this issue. This letter sets forth the interim policy which will be followed by the Commission. With respect to holiday greeting cards in 1975, the cost of purchasing and/or mailing such cards need not be reported so long as it is not paid by a campaign committee. If the cost is paid by a committee, it must be reported as an expenditure on the committee's campaign statement. Since, however, many of you already have arranged to mail your cards by means other than those prescribed in Government Code Section 84305, the procedures set forth therein need not be observed this year. Holiday cards sent by or on behalf of a state candidate need not be for- warded to the Commission this year. It should be emphasized that the interim policy stated above does not necessarily reflect the conclu- sions the Commission will reach after comprehensive consideration of all the issues surrounding mass mail- ings. However, any new policy adopted by the Commission will be applied prospectively. Any suggestions you have for treatment of mass mailings under the Political Re- form Act will be welcomed by the Commission. Seasons greetings! Sincerely, [signature] Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman DHL:jp 4
embedding.summary: FPPC Chairman Lowenstein provides interim guidance to elected officers regarding treatment of holiday greeting cards under the Political Reform Act's disclosure and mass mailing provisions. The letter establishes that card costs are reportable only if paid by campaign committees and temporarily waives procedural compliance requirements for 1975.
